symptom: Audit P5: the 'secrets are redacted at the write boundary' claim was aspirational — the central redactor covered the spine but ~10 auxiliary state stores (checkpoint tag+index, active-session pointer, schedule, review archive, memory facts, MCP/runtime descriptors+health, lane catalog) wrote caller/agent/subprocess text raw; the detector missed GitHub fine-grained/user/refresh, Google, Stripe, and PEM private-key shapes and had a second stale scanner (imports.mjs); package.json/package-lock.json (1.86/1.45) drifted 12+ versions behind version.json; and several docs miscounted gates / overclaimed doctor coverage.
consumer_impact: Canonical detector (secret-scan.mjs) gains github_pat_/ghu_/ghr_, Google AIza, Stripe [rs]k_(live|test)_, and a correct PEM matcher (complete-label backref block + unterminated-to-EOF fallback) + compound field-name awareness (refresh_token|client_secret|bearer; bare token/secret excluded to avoid eating sessionToken). New value-pattern `redactLeaves` (non-breaking on config field names) now scrubs every discrete-write state store at the boundary; imports.mjs routes through the one canonical detector. Documented residual: worker subprocess stdout/stderr LOG files (fd-direct streaming) are NOT auto-redacted. Version parity across version.json/package.json/package-lock.json enforced by a new self-test. Doc drift fixed (gate count, Rule-7 doctor wording, stale AGENTS cockpit fact, threat-model coverage).

symptom: Token/cost usage is captured (maddu usage import / maddu cost) but nothing flags a runaway session — spend stays invisible until someone looks (F5 symptom).
consumer_impact: Opt-in `cost-budget` gate: with .maddu/config/cost-budget.json {windowDays,maxTokens,metric} it sums recent tokenLedger spend and WARNs (never FAILs) when over, surfacing a runaway session in doctor/audit. Rule-5 clean (sums existing numbers, no provider call). Fleet-wide cost stays deferred (F5 accepted).

symptom: maddu learn distils corrections per-repo but they stay siloed — a lesson learned the hard way in one repo (e.g. a shell/path quirk) never reaches the next, so the fleet re-learns the same things independently.
consumer_impact: `maddu learn sync` reads sibling repos' corrections (fleet registry, local disk) and surfaces portable ones (recur in >=2 repos OR @portable), deduped + redacted; `--adopt` writes them into CLAUDE.md (approval-only) as first-class local corrections with federated provenance.

symptom: fleet upgrade delivers new framework code into installs as old as v1.15, but new code reading a projection shaped by old code could crash on a key the old shape never had — a silent, field-by-field surprise discovered one install at a time.
consumer_impact: project() stamps schemaVersion; normalizeProjection(raw) reads any old/partial/garbage projection into a total current-shape object (all keys present, nested deep-defaulted, never a throw); the can-read-old-state gate enforces the reader stays total.

symptom: maddu fleet upgrade --plan previewed a delivery but the operator still had to deliver by hand — the F1 'fixed in-tree, never received' gap was only half-closed. No safe, interlocked staged delivery across the fleet.
consumer_impact: `maddu fleet upgrade --apply` (scoped --only/--all): delivers canonical bytes to eligible behind repos by reusing the proven `maddu upgrade` engine. Per repo, sequential: re-check quiescence, snapshot managed bytes (never .maddu/events), upgrade, doctor — halt-on-red. `fleet` reclassified mutating/auto-forbidden.

symptom: maddu fleet detects who is behind but delivery was manual — the operator had to walk into each repo and run `maddu upgrade` by hand (the 'fixed in-tree, never received' gap, F1 delivery leg). No safe preview of what a fleet-wide upgrade would change or which repos are safe to touch.
consumer_impact: `maddu fleet upgrade --plan`: read-only offline preview per behind active repo — quiescence interlock (active lane claim / dirty tree / recent spine activity each block) + managed-byte delta (canonical vs recorded maddu.json hashes). The live spine is never in a delta. Mutation guarded as a follow-up.

symptom: The autonomous skill-candidate detector fired (11x/4 projects) but every downstream terminal was 0 across all 13 installs (F2): candidates are generic tag-sets (commit, test), not reusable recipes, and `maddu learn` already does the valuable auto-capture. A dead funnel carried as live.
consumer_impact: Auto-detector retired: no slice-stop auto-emit, emitFreshCandidates is a no-op, SKILL_CANDIDATE_DETECTED dispositioned dormant, allowlist no longer seeds it. Skills are hand-authored (`maddu skill create`/`from-slice`); auto-capture is `maddu learn`. The funnel-integrity gate keeps it retired.

symptom: Orchestration (coordinator/loop/pipeline/team) fires in only 2-5 of 13 installs, so a naive audit reads 'orchestration events ~= 0' as a dead domain and re-raises it as a false-alarm finding every audit (F4) — when in truth it's an opt-in advanced layer and the disciplined substrate is the real product.
consumer_impact: Every verb is tagged layer:core|orchestration in _tiers.mjs; `maddu audit positioning` frames orchestration as an opt-in fire-rate (never dead); command-tier-discipline requires a valid layer so a new verb can't be added unclassified. Charter + README reposition around the discipline loop.

symptom: maddu orient showed goal progress + a timeline but never the last gate verdict, so an operator couldn't see red/green at a glance; a failing gate surfaced as a raw stack trace, not which-gate/where/how-to-repro — friction that pushes operators to skip slices and gates.
consumer_impact: orient opens with a one-glance card (gate verdict · goal · next) and a legible GATES section (gate id + severity + spine event id + `maddu doctor --gate <id>` repro, no stack trace). GATE_RAN events persist the resolved status + event id so verdicts are exact.

symptom: The audit program's own fixes for F3/F4 were more machinery (gates, registries, verbs); unbudgeted, the enforcement surface could grow faster than dead surface retires — the cure becoming the next F3/F4.
consumer_impact: `maddu audit` (and `maddu audit budget`) now caps each governance category (gates, verbs, audit checks) from ground-truth counts vs docs/audit/governance-budget.json; over a cap FAILs unless a waiver row carries it, plus a relative self-test latency WARN.

symptom: Consumers never learned an install was stale; doctor checked integrity vs the install's own manifest, never vs latest, so a 55-versions-behind install passed green forever.
consumer_impact: doctor + orient now surface an offline staleness FLOOR (age from version.json released date) nudging `maddu upgrade`.

symptom: A consumer-impacting fix could be dogfooded in-tree but shipped to consumers as the un-fixed path, with nothing coupling the change to a release that records it.
consumer_impact: release-parity gate: an impacting change carried by a version bump must record what it delivers here; pending impacting changes WARN as delivery debt.

symptom: 34 event types accumulated as 'dead' because nothing forced a disposition when a type was defined; the dead-domain surface could only ever grow.
consumer_impact: DD1: every EVENT_TYPES key carries a definition-site disposition; a gate holds the registry in 1:1 parity; DORMANT_BY_DESIGN is derived from it. insights dead count 34 -> 0.

symptom: Version skew, liveness, and the delivery delta were invisible fleet-wide; the bridge knew every workspace path but read one repo at a time.
consumer_impact: `maddu fleet`: read-only single-machine view of every registered repo's version/currency/liveness + the version delta vs fleet latest, computed offline without running them.

symptom: The cross-project audit was a manual one-off; nothing made it recurring/self-proving, so settled findings could re-surface and a guardrail could be silently removed.
consumer_impact: audit-ledger-coherent gate: a `fixed` finding must backref a registered guardrail gate, so a guardrail can't vanish while the ledger claims the class is handled.

symptom: Everything counted events/dispositions; nothing measured whether the guardrails actually caught faults, so the edifice had no evidence it earns its weight.
consumer_impact: Outcome ledger: every gate FAIL is counted as a PREVENTED_FAULT (hard/soft by severity, per gate); the fleet view shows per-repo + active-fleet 'faults caught'.

symptom: `maddu doctor` reported a hard FAIL on a clean consumer install, telling the operator to run scripts/generate.mjs — a script that ships only in a source checkout.
consumer_impact: Gate skips on the positive source-checkout marker instead of inferring consumer-vs-source from per-target absence.

symptom: An agent built a whole feature in a fresh install with 0 sessions/lanes/slice-stops because the discipline ritual was agent-convention, not enforced.
consumer_impact: resolveSessionId precedence (flag -> env -> liveness-verified active-session cache) so one `maddu register` flows everywhere, plus `maddu hooks install` wiring SessionStart/SessionEnd.

symptom: Windows core.autocrlf rewrote all framework files to CRLF on checkout, so byte-exact integrity hashes mismatched and `upgrade` skipped every file (half-upgrade).
consumer_impact: Integrity hashing EOL-normalizes text (lossless latin1 CRLF->LF), hashes binary raw; init writes a maddu-scoped .gitattributes.

symptom: init tracked all of .maddu/ in git, so the perpetually-rewritten spine + projections churned the working tree and blocked branch switches.
consumer_impact: Policy-A .gitignore (ignore .maddu/* except durable authored artifacts) + a maddu-state-untracked gate that prints the non-destructive `git rm -r --cached` for older installs.

symptom: No CI-safe way to enforce the gates: any-red-fails would let framework gate-set churn break consumer pipelines on upgrade, so gates stayed advisory outside the terminal.
consumer_impact: maddu ci — headless LLM-free gate rail: exit nonzero only on gates the repo pinned as required (maddu ci pin; maddu.json ci.requiredGates or .maddu/config/ci.json), --strict opt-in for any-fail-is-red, GitHub Actions annotations + job summary, learn-scan advisory line. Also carries the v1.86.0→v1.87.0 docs/positioning changes (category doc 45, README governance-first reframe).

symptom: Hedged completion claims without observed proof had detection (learn scan) but no enforcement placement; gate budget stood at 69/70 with a standing next-gate-needs-retirement note.
consumer_impact: completion-claim warn gate at every slice-stop (surfaces, never blocks; >=3 cumulative + >=1 recent 30d; proof = observed GATE_RAN(ok)/verified deliverable, never self-reported flags; warn tier holds >=1 quarter before any fail promotion). Named governance-budget retirement: stress-harness-recent + upgrade-matrix-recent merged into heavy-suites-recent (same two state files, same severity) — gates stay 69/70, freed slot sole claimant declared.

symptom: Context compaction was an invisible boundary: anything an agent knew but never recorded was silently gone from model memory, and nothing in the durable record marked when/what was lost.
area: hooks/pre-compact (new fire event) + claude-hooks (PreCompact stanza) + orient (auto-announce) + doctor (stanza currency)
consumer_impact: maddu hooks install now also wires a PreCompact hook that writes a COMPACTION_CHECKPOINT (trigger, last recorded slice-stop, handoff currency) before every manual/auto compaction; orient auto-announces the latest checkpoint with no flag; doctor WARNs on partial/stale hook stanzas. Fails OPEN by design: the handler always exits 0, so compaction is never blocked. Precondition met before build: PreCompact cited in official code.claude.com hooks docs + one empirical fire test capturing the live payload.

symptom: In the framework SOURCE repo, hooks install wrote the consumer entrypoint (node maddu/bin/maddu.mjs), which does not exist there — every installed hook (SessionStart/SessionEnd/PreCompact) errored silently on fire. Surfaced by dogfooding v1.89.0 minutes after release.
area: claude-hooks (resolveHookBin) + hooks install + doctor session-hooks currency
consumer_impact: None for consumers (maddu/bin exists there; consumer default unchanged). Source-repo installs now resolve to node bin/maddu.mjs at install time; doctor staleness comparison uses the same resolution.

symptom: Claude Code auto-memory (default-on) accumulates real project knowledge outside the repo-owned record — invisible to the spine, other agents, and rebuilds.
area: learn sync --from-claude-memory (new) + vendor-memory lib + hindsight vendor kind + VENDOR_MEMORY_IMPORTED replay
consumer_impact: Import-only vendor-memory interop: preview by default, --adopt writes kind:vendor facts with provenance; content-hash-deduped (idempotent, edits import as new facts); vendor directory never modified; imports survive memory rebuild via events.

symptom: Governance mode was workspace-global: a release/stabilize window could not tighten discipline without an operator manually flipping the mode and remembering to flip it back.
area: phase (--tier, clear, PHASE_CLEARED) + governance readEffectiveGovernance + consumers (loops, coordinator, strict-approval, trust, governance show)
consumer_impact: Sterile phases: phase set --tier <mode> escalates the effective governance mode while the phase is active (escalation-only, never weakens; explicit overrides keep winning); phase clear lifts it. Base governance.json untouched — the overlay is computed per read.

symptom: The v1.87-v1.91 governance domains existed only in the CLI: the cockpit rendered COMPACTION_CHECKPOINT/VENDOR_MEMORY_IMPORTED/PHASE_* events unclassified with empty summaries, the memory kind filter offered neither correction nor vendor, and the brief card hid an active phase tier.
area: cockpit (event-rows classify+summarize, learning kind filter, orientation brief phase-tier line) + snapshot harness /bridge/orientation fixture
consumer_impact: Cockpit surfaces the new domains: compaction checkpoints and phase events classified into the framework color family with human summaries (trigger + slice-stop anchor), vendor imports in the knowledge family, memory filterable by correction/vendor kinds, and the brief card shows the active sterile-phase tier.

symptom: Governance-tier decisions had no evidence surface: nothing computed whether a lane's verified record (clean slice-stops, green gates, no hedged claims) supported relaxing or demanded reverting a tier — trust was operator intuition, and GATE_RAN events carried no session/lane attribution for any future scorer to bind on.
area: earned autonomy: lib/autonomy.mjs scorer + commands/autonomy.mjs verb + AUTONOMY_SCORED/AUTONOMY_RECOMMENDATION spine events + GATE_RAN attribution enrichment (gates.mjs/slice-stop.mjs) + orient/governance-show surfacing
consumer_impact: maddu autonomy grades each lane's earned trust deterministically (Wilson lower bound over witnessed-clean vs dirty outcomes, 3-rung ladder, daily clean-credit cap) and recommends — never applies — tier changes; recommendations are muted while any phase is active; slice-stop-run gates are now session-stamped so exact attribution accumulates from this release.

symptom: Earned autonomy (v1.92.0) existed only in the CLI: the cockpit rendered AUTONOMY_SCORED/AUTONOMY_RECOMMENDATION unclassified with empty summaries and the orientation brief card had no trust-ladder surface.
area: cockpit (event-rows classify+summarize, orientation brief Autonomy row) + autonomy projection slot + buildOrientation + snapshot-harness orientation fixture
consumer_impact: Cockpit surfaces earned autonomy: both event types classified into the trust family with human summaries (scored digest, rung-change arrows, muted marker), and the orientation brief card shows the latest live recommendation as its own row.

symptom: Focus Director tagged detailed, honest, on-goal slice summaries as away (0.85-0.99) and raised two spurious DRIFT_FLAGGED on 2026-07-03: the drift metric divided goal-token intersection by the focus text's own size, punishing verbosity as distance, compounded by no stemming and by success-condition verification vocabulary polluting the goal token set.
area: focus director: lib/focus.mjs (tokenize stemming, goalTokens objective+constraints only, tagTurn absolute-anchor metric + anchors/anchorHits signals) + docs/43
consumer_impact: Drift tags now measure whether the goal's distinctive terms appear in current attention (>=2 anchors toward, 1 lateral, 0 away) instead of penalizing detail; every FOCUS_TAGGED event carries the matched anchors so a tag is self-explanatory; regression fixture pins the real 2026-07-03 false positives AND the true positive.

symptom: Root resolution walked up to the NEAREST .maddu/ directory, so any command run inside a future lane worktree (.maddu/worktrees/<lane>/ carries its own tracked .maddu/ copy) would bind spine appends, sessions, and gates to the checkout copy instead of the primary repo record — a split spine. Additionally, CLI lane claim accepted arbitrary lane strings that phase-4 attach would have interpolated into filesystem paths and git branch refs.
area: lib/paths.mjs (resolveRoots work/state split, .maddu-state-root pointer, MADDU_STATE_ROOT env) + commands/_spine.mjs (resolveRepoRoot binds STATE root; resolveWorkAndStateRoots) + commands/slice-stop.mjs (git diff + deliverables scoped to WORK root) + new lib/worktrees.mjs (LANE_SLUG_RE SSOT, branch encoding, containment-checked worktree paths) + bridge-routes-lanes.mjs (imports the SSOT regex)
consumer_impact: Commands run inside a redirected checkout (pointer file or MADDU_STATE_ROOT) append to the primary spine while diffing the local checkout; broken pointers fail loudly instead of silently splitting the record; lane ids destined for worktree paths/branch refs are validated by one shared rule. No behavior change for repos without redirection markers.
ledger_ref: roadmap #12a phases 1-2 (plan pln_20260705002732_7773); Codex consult P1 #1-#2

symptom: The spine's verified record could not be consumed as normalized experience: no trajectory/step view existed, outcome evidence (gates, reviews, learn-scan) was never attached to the work it judged, improvement suggestions required re-deriving evidence by hand each time, and sharing any slice of the record outside the repo had no governed, redaction-gated path.
area: EXP phases 0-6: lib/experience.mjs (deriveExperience: steps=event ids, session-linkage-only trajectories + honest env, late-bound signals via explicit-ref/gate-window(autonomy-verbatim)/derived learn-scan(nowMs:null)/trajectory-scope) + lib/evolve.mjs + commands/evolve.mjs (4 deterministic detectors, >=3-occurrence/>=2-scope thresholds, content-addressed recIds, prior-art dedup vs the exact adopt concatenation, recommend-never-apply, honest no-op; adopt routes ONLY through existing write paths) + lib/learn-spine.mjs + learn --spine (shared pair extraction: TOOL_REFUSED->COMPLETED, GATE_RAN fail->ok) + lib/experience-export.mjs + experience export --format atdp (refuse-on-hit secret gate with structurally NO skip flag, realpath-basis repo+framework-state confinement incl. symlink/junction bypass closed, non-ATDP overwrite guard, atomic write, deterministic no-clock bytes, --until reproducibility, trainingEligibility:false hard) + GET /bridge/experience + cockpit #experience route + docs/50-experience-evolve.md both trees
consumer_impact: maddu experience [list|show|stats] renders the spine as session trajectories of typed steps with late-bound outcome signals (zero writes, zero new event types, deterministic); maddu evolve plan/adopt gives evidence-gated recommendations the operator explicitly adopts through already-audited write paths - thin evidence yields an honest no-op, never manufactured findings; learn run/digest --spine adds the spine as a second learn corpus; experience export produces a deterministic, redaction-gated ATDP artifact that refuses on any secret-shaped value with no override flag and reproduces byte-identically (--until pins past ranges), trainingEligibility:false; the cockpit gains a read-only #experience route in the Verify cluster.
ledger_ref: EXP roadmap (plan pln_20260706063019_b7e8) phases 0-6; PRs #227 (design), #232 (P1-P3), #233 (P4), #234/#235 (P5+P6); per-phase adversarial red-teams to CLEAN

symptom: Teams running an SLM factory (curate -> fine-tune -> benchmark -> promote) had no neutral record between the steps and no governance on the hand-offs: dataset/training/eval/promotion facts lived in scattered tool outputs, a checkpoint could reach production on a declared (unverified) stage, no approval was structurally required to advance toward release, and no repo-owned gates held licenses, secrets, split-freezes, regressions, or rollback plans.
area: SLM-governance phases 0-6: lib/model-manifests.mjs (5-kind validators + single-read hash-pinning ingest w/ key-level secret sweep) + contract 1.1.0 (+11 MODEL_* types, frozen schemas, dormant dispositions, verify.mjs promotion-integrity rules: derived stage, exact approval binding, strictly-downward rollback) + commands/model.mjs (verb #72; always-on stage-keyed approval ride, cascade only for candidate; CLI mirrors verifier FAILs) + gates/model-pack (12 operator-owned ci-pinnable gates via model gates install, edit-preserving, fail-closed skips) + lib/model-projection.mjs + cockpit #model + GET /bridge/model + docs 51 both trees + README use case 7
consumer_impact: New opt-in capability, byte-identical default path: repos not running `maddu model` see zero behavior change (all MODEL_* rules live inside the referential switch; the model registry derives read-time, no state-file change). Factory repos get hash-pinned manifests on the spine, a promotion ladder that cannot be claimed past (derived stage + approvals ride, no auto-decide above candidate), and a 12-gate starter pack in .maddu/gates/ that is theirs to edit and pin. Contract minor-bumped 1.0.0->1.1.0 (additive only); zero new dependencies.
ledger_ref: plan pln_20260706133422_0f60 + PRs #237-#244 + design docs/research/slm-governance-design.md (census WINS; red-teamed 4 rounds) + every phase subagent-red-teamed to CLEAN pre-merge

symptom: 63 of 201 spine-append sites persisted operator/agent/worker free text unredacted (session focus/handoffs, slice-stop prose, inbox, plan/goal/loop text, learn/vendor facts, advisor prompts; bridge routes spread entire POST bodies into events), and four local stores (mailbox bodies, briefing originals, skill bodies, wrapper error log) held free text no gate ever saw — a pasted secret became a permanent record, and with team-sync the whole data payload becomes git-visible.
area: payload-redaction phases 1-6: lib/secret-scan.mjs redactDataPayload (scan-first/clone-only-on-hit, key-aware value-under-sensitive-key rule, keys never dropped) + spine.append central sweep (flat + partitioned + ENOENT retry; prev_hash over stored bytes) + appendTokenUsage bypass sweep + mailbox/briefings/skills/logWrapperError self-sweeps + hard-rules/49-team-sync/37-failure-learning honesty docs + scripts/test/payload-redaction.mjs (37) + refuse-gate tests converted to raw historic-line planting
consumer_impact: Every spine event data payload now passes the canonical redactor at write time — no emit site, present or future, can persist a raw pattern-matched or key-named secret; clean events are byte- and reference-identical (0 clones/0 byte changes over the 2,964-event live spine). Write-time only: pre-existing events stay as-written and a dirty historic spine still refuses spine sync init (the export/sync refuse-gates remain history defense). worker-logs/<id>.log stays raw by purpose (local-only). Zero new dependencies; contract unchanged at 1.1.0.
ledger_ref: plan pln_20260706215157_42d3 + PRs #245-#247 + design docs/research/worker-payload-redaction-design.md (untracked-local; census 201/63) + design red-team SOUND-WITH-CONDITIONS + impl red-teams CLEAN (round-2 P2 = invisible skill bodies, fixed in code)

symptom: Máddu asked agents to register a session, claim a lane, declare a plan/goal, slice-stop, and commit — but only NUDGED. An agent that ignored the ritual built with no session/lane/slice records and nothing stopped it (hard rule #8 only bites on lane contention). The framework's own agent had recently drifted off the ritual across a multi-slice refresh, which is exactly what Máddu exists to prevent.
area: self-discipline enforcement: template/maddu/runtime/lib/discipline.mjs (pure decide() ordered blockers session->lane->goal/plan->slice-stop->commit; classifyBashWrite() dequote+WRITE-precedence+exec-wrapper; per-session nextCounter()/enforcePreTool() FAIL-OPEN; lastOwnSliceStop()) + commands/hooks.mjs pre-tool-use (resolve session -> auto-claim -> enforce -> deny/nudge, never exits 2) + claude-hooks.mjs matcher += Bash + governance.mjs discipline-enforcement key + commands/_gates-before-done.mjs (goal done/plan complete) + gates/builtin/discipline-observed.mjs + hooks uninstall off-switch + docs 30/44
consumer_impact: With the hooks installed (maddu hooks install), a mutating edit is now BLOCKED when a session ritual is stale, tier-scaled by governance (strict=block / standard=graduated warn->block / relaxed=nudge). Fails open (any evaluator/git error -> allow; the hook never exits 2 so it cannot crash a tool), never gates the remedy commands (slice-stop/goal/plan/lane claim, git commit) so a block cannot deadlock its own fix, and never auto-performs a ritual (block-and-tell). goal done/plan complete refuse at strict while a required gate is red. Opt-in and Claude-Code-specific; no new verb (72/72), no contract bump. Repos without the hook installed see zero change.
ledger_ref: plan pln_20260708225427_4c7f + PR #263 + directive [[project_self_discipline_enforcement_gap]]; twice Codex-reviewed on design, three adversarial Codex rounds pre-merge (cross-session/truncation counter reset + commit-trailer deadlock all closed) -> SAFE TO MERGE

symptom: The person accountable for an agent's work often can't read the code to see what it did — and the record, though complete, spoke in event types, not in what-happened. There was no non-technical readout of what an agent was fed vs WITHHELD (and why), whether it stayed on the operator's goal, or how work was faring across many repos at once.
area: Operator Plane + oversight (read-only): shared success-eval + maddu status --line + opt-in hooks --statusline; orient --digest 'while you were away' + cockpit; single-project cockpit route; decision-ledger route (per-row sha ties to the tamper chain); enriched handoff (display-time fusion); cross-workspace portfolio wall + needs-the-human (/bridge/_all/portfolio fan-out); oversight route + maddu spine oversight (skillRefusals fed-vs-withheld + plain-language why). Cockpit routes 46->50.
consumer_impact: New read-only operator surfaces, all contract-neutral (contract 1.3.0), verbs 72/72, no new state files (display-time projections; the bridge reads the CLI-cached success-eval). maddu status --line gives a one-line on-goal/drift segment (opt-in status-line); orient --digest summarizes what changed while away; new cockpit routes #oversight/#digest/#project/#decisions/#portfolio; /bridge/_all/portfolio surfaces every workspace's state and which need a human. Accountability substrate for non-coders, not a safety proof.
ledger_ref: Operator Plane PRs #254-#259 (plan) + oversight PR #253 ([[project_operator_plane_harvest]], [[project_oversight_surface]]); harvested from the snyggare.se/maddu operator mocks

symptom: An auto-injectable skill could be pointed at an unreviewed URL (a swap between audit time and inject time), and CLI-first work accreted stale sessions + orphaned lane claims that the bridge-only janitor never reached.
area: guardrails + coordination: skill URL-swap closure (SKILL_INJECTION_REFUSED, trusted/local-only auto-injection, skill-no-external-refs gate) + self-cleaning lanes/sessions (SessionStart sweep via janitor:sessions trigger, orphaned-claim release) + goal lifecycle tidy
consumer_impact: Auto-injected skills must be locally resident / trusted — an injection whose source changed is refused and recorded, closing the audit-time-vs-inject-time gap. Stale sessions past the threshold auto-close (releasing their claims) and orphaned lane claims are released on SessionStart, so the record stays clean without a running bridge. Rule-#9 clean (allowlisted triggers).
ledger_ref: PRs #251 (skill URL-swap), #252 (self-cleaning lanes/sessions + goal lifecycle); cockpit design refresh + monolith decomposition #260-#262 ship no consumer-behavior change (cockpit is read-only)

symptom: A 2026-07-09 seven-auditor flaw audit (Codex-verified) found several headline guarantees passing for the wrong reason: viewing a mailbox message ran attacker JS against an unauthenticated bridge that addressed every mounted repo (cockpit stored-XSS + no bridge auth + no cross-workspace binding); the 'tamper-evident' spine only WARNed on a hash mismatch (so `spine verify` stayed exit-0 on a tampered spine) while chain-STRIPPING produced zero issues at all; and self-discipline enforcement had SILENT bypasses — `node -e` interpreter writes classified allow, `maddu hooks uninstall` / `governance set-override discipline-enforcement off` self-disabled enforcement with no reason and no record, and every fail-open wrote nothing — the actor could be the sole witness to its own rewrite OR silently turn the witness off.
area: audit remediation P0-P3: cockpit innerHTML sinks escaped to text nodes + markdown safeHref (P0a); bridge capability-token auth (per-boot secret, timing-safe, method-primary mutating guard + cross-workspace check, BRIDGE_CROSS_WORKSPACE) (P0b); spine tamper-DETECTING — a post-cutover (>=1.98.0/SPINE_CUTOVER) chain FAILs on interior edit/delete/insert/reorder/strip via a unified locked+chained flat-append primitive shared by spine.append() and the token wrapper, chained init prefix, and an empty-partition SPINE_CUTOVER seed (P1); discipline honesty — a 5-class Bash classifier (write/self-disable/remedy/ambiguous/read; WRITE dominates, interpreter -e/-c payload probe, separator normalization) + governance-mode policy, DISCIPLINE_SKIPPED/ENFORCEMENT_ERROR witnesses at the hook seam (discipline.mjs stays spine-less), a write-ahead + abort-on-append-failure off-switch (domain-checked, reason-required, strict-approval-gated, GOVERNANCE_OVERRIDE_CHANGED), a hooks-uninstall witness, null-session gate + Claude-SID counter fallback, and a discipline-observed out-of-band config provenance replay (P2); verification not actor-witness — success-eval + project/self/heavy test recency now derive from a tamper-detecting VERIFICATION_STARTED/RAN spine RECEIPT appended from the runner's IN-PROCESS result and read back through a verified (parse-clean, uncapped, collectEvents) read, never a hand-writable state file; success readouts refuse 'goal met' from a stale/goal-mismatched/future-ts/broken-chain receipt (counts move under lastKnown), the GET stays non-authoritative (readAllStrict + last spine-integrity verdict → ok/broken/unknown, no spawn); recency gates enforce U2 pairing (exactly-one kind+profile STARTED↔RAN) + U1 dangling-window=TTL; completion-claim broadened to confident verification-claims (subject+outcome ADJACENCY, negation/quotation-suppressed, lane-bound family-matched proof, machinery-gated so pre-P3 claims aren't policed) (P3). | P0-P4 (P4 = gate scope + CI integrity, PR #271): rule-5-no-provider-sdks scanned a NONEXISTENT <root>/maddu (0 files -> green-because-wrong-scope) -> dual-layout resolver + per-subtree sentinels + scanned==0 FAIL + a lexically-aware banned-import matcher (dynamic/scoped/module.require/optional-call; HTML per <script>+on* handler; CSS/JSX excluded); SKIP was counted as PASS (exit 0) -> reserved skip exit 77 for declared-skippable tasks only + --fail-on-skip + taskSkipped + honest receipt; 19/66 pinned required gates were severity:warn (could never red) -> ci pin fail-capable-only + fail-closed required-resolution + promote maddu-state-untracked/dependency-freshness (repin 66->52); command-tier-discipline verifies tier CORRECTNESS not just presence (spine read-only->mutating, own-file mutation WARN); maddu-ci.yml npm ci + Chromium job + --fail-on-skip + an aggregate ci required-check job; and a Linux-only spine.append pending-during-sync-init race (concurrent append LOST) fixed to retry.
consumer_impact: Contract 1.3.0 -> 1.7.0 (added BRIDGE_CROSS_WORKSPACE, SPINE_CUTOVER, DISCIPLINE_SKIPPED, ENFORCEMENT_ERROR, GOVERNANCE_OVERRIDE_CHANGED, VERIFICATION_STARTED, VERIFICATION_RAN; additive). `maddu spine verify` now EXITS NONZERO (and the spine-integrity gate reds, even when capped) on an after-the-fact edit/deletion/insertion/prev_hash-strip of a post-cutover chain — pre-cutover and legacy history stay lenient (chain_fork/chain_gap WARN) so existing spines never false-FAIL. Every flat write now carries prev_hash. Bridge mutations require the per-boot capability token (loopback-CSRF boundary). Discipline: with the hooks installed, an interpreter write (`node -e "fs.writeFileSync…"`) is now gated like an edit; disabling enforcement (`hooks uninstall`, `set-override discipline-enforcement off/nudge`) is domain-checked, requires --reason, is refused under strict without --approve, and is recorded WRITE-AHEAD (a disable that can't be recorded aborts — a disable is never silent); a bypass / fail-open / off-switch change always leaves a DISCIPLINE_SKIPPED / ENFORCEMENT_ERROR / GOVERNANCE_OVERRIDE_CHANGED witness; an out-of-band config edit that weakens enforcement is surfaced by `maddu doctor`. Verification: a goal-met readout, a project/self-test recency gate, and the heavy-suites gate now believe a VERIFIED spine receipt, not a hand-writable `.maddu/state/*-last-run.json` (writing `{fail:0,ts:now}` no longer turns a recency gate green); on the bridge GET the goal readout is receipt-backed + time-bounded + integrity-labelled (never spawns); `maddu test`/`self-test`/stress/upgrade-matrix emit the receipt pair; a claimed 'all gates green' slice-stop with no matching-family proof in its lane is nudged. Detection stays deliberately incomplete for shell-wrapped/opaque writes and unkeyed forged-but-consistent receipts (the OS's job); no new gate/verb (74/72 budget held — the verification guard is an auto-discovered self-test). Claim reworded evident->detecting, unkeyed. P4: `maddu ci` reds honestly (a warn-severity or unresolved pinned gate is itself red; required==fail-capable); `maddu self-test --fail-on-skip` reds instead of greening a skipped gate; the source-repo CI runs npm ci so the cockpit/golden gates actually execute, with a single aggregate `ci` required status check on main (merge waits for Actions). rule-5 now scans the real framework tree (template/maddu/+commands/+bin/) and catches dynamic/scoped/CommonJS provider imports; `spine` is correctly mutating. A concurrent spine.append during `spine sync init` no longer throws/loses the event. No new gate/verb (74/72); one back-compatible self-test report field.
ledger_ref: PRs #266 (P0a XSS), #267 (P0b bridge auth), #268 (P1 spine tamper), #269 (P2 discipline honesty), #270 (P3), #271 (P4); roadmap ~/.claude/plans/fuzzy-herding-pumpkin.md; each tier's plan + diff Codex-reviewed before self-merge — P3 plan 6 Codex rounds to SOUND ([[project_maddu_system_audit_2026-07-09]])
